title: Parkio Daily for May 20, 2026
teaser: Millennium Falcon gets a new mission tomorrow, Disneyland drops park hopping restrictions June 9, and a new Cinderella Castle reveal.

rides.name: Space Mountain
rides.note: Morning waits tend to stay low before lunch crowds arrive

rides.name: Radiator Springs Racers
rides.note: Early entry typically offers the shortest lines of the day

rides.name: Rise of the Resistance
rides.note: Virtual queue opens at park entry; best odds in first minute

breaking.title: New Millennium Falcon Mission Launches Tomorrow with Mando and Grogu
breaking.body: Millennium Falcon: Smugglers Run debuts an updated mission May 22 at both Disneyland and Hollywood Studios, teaming guests with the Mandalorian and Grogu to track bounties across Tatooine, Cloud City, Coruscant, and the Endor wreckage. Flight crews take on pilot, gunner, or engineer roles in the updated experience.

parkioInsight.text: Galaxy's Edge tends to draw heavy crowds on new-mission launch days. Riding Smugglers Run within the first two hours of park opening usually keeps waits under 30 minutes.

breaking.title: Disneyland Drops 11 AM Park Hopping Restriction June 9
breaking.body: Starting June 9, Disneyland Resort guests with Park Hopper tickets or Magic Keys can move between Disneyland and California Adventure at any time, ending the 11 AM wait rule in place since reopening. Guests still need a park reservation indicating their starting park.

parkioInsight.text: This change opens early-morning hopping windows. Guests can now rope-drop one park, then hop for lunch or mid-morning attractions in the other before mid-day crowds peak.

bignews.title: BDX Droids Return to Galaxy's Edge at Disneyland May 22
bignews.body: Three BDX droids—Red, Oskar, and Grek—will begin appearing at Black Spire Station in Disneyland's Galaxy's Edge starting tomorrow. The playful droids-in-training roam the land, showing off unique personalities as they take on new challenges.

parkioInsight.text: Character interactions in Galaxy's Edge tend to draw clusters of guests. Catching the droids early in the morning or late afternoon near Black Spire Station often offers shorter wait times for photos.

bignews.title: Cinderella Castle Repaint Nearly Complete at Magic Kingdom
bignews.body: Magic Kingdom has confirmed its Cinderella Castle makeover is almost done, restoring the icon to a more classic, refined color scheme. The project removes the bright blue-and-pink accents added during the 50th anniversary celebration.

parkioInsight.text: Photos in front of the castle are easiest to grab early morning or late evening when crowds thin. Main Street tends to be clearest within 30 minutes of park opening.

bignews.title: Disney Launches $399 Private PhotoPass Shoots at Select Resorts
bignews.body: Walt Disney World is piloting Signature Portrait Sessions, offering private 20-minute photo shoots with a PhotoPass photographer at select resort locations for $399. The sessions include digital downloads and are bookable in advance.

parkioInsight.text: Resort photo sessions tend to offer quieter backdrops than in-park shoots. Booking early-morning slots usually avoids other guests in the background and provides softer lighting.

topstories.title: ABC Commissary Sees Guest Praise Turnaround at Hollywood Studios
topstories.body: The quick-service spot once labeled a skip by many guests is earning positive reviews for updated menu items, including a raspberry pistachio cheesecake and improved entrees. The location offers indoor seating and shorter lines than nearby Docking Bay 7.

parkioInsight.text: ABC Commissary tends to be quieter during lunch than Docking Bay or Woody's Lunch Box. Arriving between 11:30 and noon often keeps wait times under 10 minutes.

topstories.title: Rock 'n' Roller Coaster Easter Eggs Honor MuppetVision 3D Legacy
topstories.body: Disney has released a guide to Easter eggs hidden throughout the newly rethemed Rock 'n' Roller Coaster Starring the Muppets, including nods to the shuttered MuppetVision 3D theater and past Muppets park appearances. Full POV videos are now online.

parkioInsight.text: Guests looking for Easter egg details should ride early or late in the day when queue lines move faster. Mid-morning and early afternoon tend to see the heaviest waits.

topstories.title: LEGO Unveils $400 Main Street USA Set with 4,900 Pieces
topstories.body: LEGO and Disney Parks have announced a massive Main Street USA set featuring the iconic Disneyland and Magic Kingdom entrance land. The highly detailed build includes storefronts, streetscapes, and miniature characters.

parkioInsight.text: High-demand LEGO sets tied to Disney Parks often sell out fast at Emporium and World of Disney. Shopping within the first hour of park opening typically offers the best stock.

icymi.title: SeaWorld Orlando Hits 43,000 Animal Rescues Milestone
icymi.body: SeaWorld's year-round rescue operation has now saved more than 43,000 marine animals, including the 43,000th rescue—a California sea lion pup found in Carlsbad. The federally recognized program operates 24/7 across all three SeaWorld locations.

icymi.title: Space 220 Debuts Updated Dinner Menu at EPCOT
icymi.body: The popular EPCOT restaurant has refreshed its evening menu with new entrees and updated plating. Reservations remain difficult to secure, with availability often appearing 60 days out.

spotlight.title: Shadows of Memory Skywalker Saga Show Illuminates Galaxy's Edge Nightly
spotlight.body: This projection-mapping spectacular transforms the spires of Batuu into a canvas for the Skywalker family story, featuring stunning visuals and iconic Star Wars music. The show runs nightly near the Millennium Falcon, with the best views from multiple vantage points including Droid Depot and Black Spire Surplus.

parkioInsight.text: Staking out a viewing spot 20–30 minutes before showtime usually offers clear sightlines. The area near the Falcon tends to fill fastest, while spots near Droid Depot often stay open longer.
